header: From first edit to finished site — in three slides
subheadline: Edit inline in the Visual Editor, publish a polished light theme, flip to dark without touching a line of CSS. One package does all three.

slides.title: Edit the page on the page
slides.lead: The Visual Editor turns the TYPO3 backend into the site itself — click, type, done.
slides.description: Editors work directly on the rendered page with the live theme applied. No guessing from gray records, no preview round-trips — what they see is what ships.

image.title: Editing in the Desiderio Visual Editor
image.alt: TYPO3 Visual Editor with a Desiderio page being edited inline.
image.credit: Screenshot of the Visual Editor with Desiderio installed.

slides.title: Publish design-system polish
slides.lead: The rendered site looks like a design agency built it — because a design system did.
slides.description: Every one of the 255 elements draws from the same OKLCH tokens, so headlines, cards, and buttons stay perfectly on palette. This is the Lagoon preset, untouched.

image.title: Desiderio frontend in the Lagoon preset
image.alt: Rendered Desiderio frontend hero page in the light Lagoon theme preset.
image.credit: Screenshot of a live Desiderio frontend page.

slides.title: Dark mode, already done
slides.lead: Light and dark live in the same token set — flip the switch and every element follows.
slides.description: No second stylesheet, no forgotten corners: the Midnight preset renders this pricing page in WCAG 2.2-checked contrast, straight out of the box.

image.title: Desiderio pricing page in the dark Midnight preset
image.alt: Rendered Desiderio pricing page in the dark Midnight theme preset.
image.credit: Screenshot of a live Desiderio frontend page in dark mode.
